taxonomy.gloss: Point of view as moat, small & sharp

content_themes.example_quote: Shipped a new Button component to the design system today. 14 props, 6 variants, and a 40-line migration guide. This is the job now.
Three meetings to align on a border radius. I miss when building meant building.
Reminder: a div with an onClick is not a button. Your keyboard users can't reach it. #a11y

years.voice_reading: A buttoned-up professional register. 42% of tweets carry a hashtag and 58% a link — this is announcement-and-resource mode, broadcasting work outward rather than conversing (only 5 replies all year). Sentences run long (~16 words) and emoji are near-absent; the voice is competent, a little weary, and clearly speaking to a peer audience of frontend engineers.

notable_tweets.text: Three meetings to align on a border radius. I miss when building meant building.
notable_tweets.why: First crack in the corporate facade — the frustration that later drives the pivot.

content_themes.example_quote: Spent the weekend building a tiny tool to track my reading. No roadmap, no Jira, no standup. Felt like myself again.
Half the complexity at work exists to support a scale we don't have yet.
Still maintaining the design system, but my heart's in the side projects now.

years.voice_reading: The voice loosens. Hashtags fall from 42% to 23% and replies more than double — she's starting to talk with people, not just broadcast. Questions tick up (15%), reflecting a year of doubt. Still long-ish sentences and almost no emoji, but the topics drift from the design system toward what she does on nights and weekends.

notable_tweets.text: Half the complexity at work exists to support a scale we don't have yet.
notable_tweets.why: The anti-scale thesis that becomes a core belief in the indie era.

content_themes.example_quote: Quit BigCorp today. Three years of process. I want to feel the whole loop again — idea to user — without a Jira ticket in between.
Hot take: most 'scalability' problems are imaginary. You will not have a million users. Build for the ten you have.
The cloud was a mistake for small apps. Your data should live on your device. Sync is a feature, not a foundation.
First indie app shipped in a weekend. A tiny offline notes thing. 12 users. I have never been happier.

years.voice_reading: The pivot year, and the metrics show it: hashtags collapse to 9%, exclamations jump to 14%, sentences shorten to ~13 words. This is the punchy, energized voice of someone who just got their freedom back. Vocabulary richness drops as she begins circling a tight set of recurring slogans (the loop, build for the ten, local-first).

entities.name: @devpeer_demo
entities.relationship: now a fellow indie, traded notes

notable_tweets.text: Quit BigCorp today. Three years of process. I want to feel the whole loop again — idea to user — without a Jira ticket in between.
notable_tweets.why: The hinge of the entire account.

notable_tweets.text: First indie app shipped in a weekend. A tiny offline notes thing. 12 users. I have never been happier.
notable_tweets.why: Sets the small-and-happy template for the indie era.

years.era_label: Indie hacker: local-first + build in public

content_themes.example_quote: local-first isn't nostalgia. it's the only way to build software that respects the person using it. own your data, own your tools.
Shipping in public changed everything for me. Every bug fix is a tweet. Every user is a conversation. The build IS the marketing.
Crossed $2k MRR on the notes app today. From 12 users to 800 paying in 8 months. Small and sharp beats big and generic, every time.
the moat was never the tech. it's having a point of view. generic tools are already free.

years.voice_reading: Peak indie voice. Hashtags are effectively gone (1.2%) while replies surge to 59 — she's running a conversation, not a broadcast. The lowest vocabulary richness of any year (0.24) is not carelessness but consolidation: she's repeating a tight doctrine (local-first, build-in-public, small & sharp) until it lands. Short, punchy, exclamation-flecked.

entities.name: @devpeer_demo
entities.relationship: peer she publicly compares notes with

notable_tweets.text: Crossed $2k MRR on the notes app today. From 12 users to 800 paying in 8 months. Small and sharp beats big and generic, every time.
notable_tweets.why: Proof the small-and-sharp thesis paid off.

content_themes.example_quote: software is just a way to hand someone your point of view. Make it personal or don't bother.
You don't get ready first. You start, and the readiness shows up later.
I preach 'build for the ten users you have' but I refresh my MRR chart ten times a day. Growth brain is hard to quit.

years.voice_reading: The voice matures into aphorism. Hashtags are essentially zero, sentences settle back toward ~14 words as she writes more reflective one-liners than punchy announcements. Replies stay high (51) — she now spends real energy encouraging others. Notably, she begins turning the skepticism on herself, naming the gap between her anti-growth creed and her MRR-watching.

notable_tweets.text: software is just a way to hand someone your point of view. Make it personal or don't bother.
notable_tweets.why: The thesis the whole arc was building toward.

notable_tweets.text: I preach 'build for the ten users you have' but I refresh my MRR chart ten times a day. Growth brain is hard to quit.
notable_tweets.why: Self-aware enough to flag her own contradiction — the trait that makes the voice trustworthy.
